header.description: Networking Equipment Selector knowledge base for the Cisco Catalyst 9200 Series switches. Pass 1 of 2: this file carries the catalog (atomic SKU facts) and groups (named shared compatibility sets) — the reusable scaffolding. Switch models are added in Pass 2; until then 'models' is empty. Scope: C9200 (modular uplink) and C9200L (fixed uplink). The compact C9200CX sub-family is deferred to a later pass (fixed internal PSU / UPOE / HVDC do not fit the current PSU schema); Meraki (C9200L-M) is out of scope. Structure: catalog + groups + models.

sources.name: Cisco Catalyst 9200 Series Switches Data Sheet

design_notes: Pass 1 of 2: catalog + groups only. 'models' is empty until Pass 2; the per-model validator checks (valid_primary subset, poe_budget_matrix max, access-port sum == total_port_count) therefore do not yet run. The standalone group-integrity and catalog port-speed checks DO run.

Scope: C9200 (modular uplink, FRU PSU + FRU fans, StackWise-160) and C9200L (fixed uplink, FRU PSU + fixed fans, StackWise-80). C9200CX (compact, fanless, fixed internal PSU, UPOE/UPOE+/HVDC) is deferred to its own pass. Meraki C9200L-M is out of scope.

Licensing: DNA-term regime only, tier locked by the -E/-A hardware suffix (Network Essentials/Advantage perpetual embedded + a mandatory-attach 3/5/7-yr DNA subscription). The newer 'Cisco Catalyst Software' (DNX) subscription printed in OG Tables 12-15 is intentionally dropped, mirroring the C9300 treatment.

Power supplies: AC only. The DC options (PWR-C5-715WDC / PWR-C6-715WDC, spare-only, PoE-models-only per datasheet Table 4) are intentionally omitted — never proposed in practice. With DC gone, each model's valid_primary is a single AC SKU (fixed by the model's port/PoE class per datasheet Table 2), and the poe_budget_matrix expresses single vs redundant (identical second PSU), not a wattage choice.
No StackPower on the C9200 series (data stacking only, StackWise-160 / StackWise-80). stackpower_cables and stackpower_cable_groups are intentionally empty.

Network-module compatibility (OG Table 3 footnotes): C9200-NM-2Y and C9200-NM-2Q are supported only on C9200-24PXG / C9200-48PXG; C9200-NM-4G is not supported on those PXG models. Hence two module groups (c9200-modular-uplink-modules vs c9200-pxg-uplink-modules). Fixed-uplink C9200L models carry their uplinks inline (Pass 2), so they reference no module group.

The *-NONE none-option values (C9200-SPS-NONE, C9200-STACK-NONE, etc.) are configurator constructs (CCW 'remove the category' selections), not catalog SKUs and not validated against the catalog — matching the C9300 convention. C9200-NM-BLANK is the real OG-sourced blank network module.

Series-specific groups (PSU, stack cables) are kept as their own named groups per project convention even where a SKU or cable resembles another sub-family: C9200 (Config-6 PWR-C6-*) and C9200L (Config-5 PWR-C5-*) do not share power supplies, and each uses its own stack kit (C9200-STACK-KIT vs C9200L-STACK-KIT).

catalog._comment: Atomic SKU facts. One row per real orderable component. What a thing IS, independent of which switch uses it.

network_modules.notes: Datasheet Table 3 '2 x 25G network module'. Speed set (1/10/25G) taken from the datasheet Table 2 C9200L-*PXG-2Y '2x 1/10/25G' fixed-uplink description (same SFP28 optics). OG Table 3 footnote: supported only on C9200-24PXG / C9200-48PXG.
Datasheet Table 3 '2 x 40G network module'. OG Table 3 footnote: supported only on C9200-24PXG / C9200-48PXG.
Datasheet Table 3 '4x 1G network module'. OG Table 3 footnote: NOT supported on C9200-24PXG / C9200-48PXG.
Datasheet Table 3 '4x 1G/10G network module'. No compatibility footnote — supported on all modular C9200 models.

power_supplies.notes: 125W AC Config-6 (OG Table 5). C9200 modular series. Data-model default primary (datasheet Table 2).
600W AC Config-6 (OG Table 5). C9200 modular series. 24-port PoE / 48-partial-PoE default primary (datasheet Table 2/4).
1KW AC Config-6 (OG Table 5). C9200 modular series. 48-port full-PoE default primary (datasheet Table 2/4).
125W AC Config-5 (OG Table 5). C9200L fixed-uplink series. Data-model default primary (datasheet Table 2).
600W AC Config-5 (OG Table 5). C9200L fixed-uplink series. 24-port PoE / 48-partial-PoE default primary (datasheet Table 2/4).
1KW AC Config-5 (OG Table 5). C9200L fixed-uplink series. 48-port full-PoE default primary (datasheet Table 2/4).

stack_cables.notes: Data stack 50 cm cable, default with the stack kit (datasheet Table 6). SKU name and datasheet say T4; OG Table 4 description column labels it 'Type 3 Stacking Cable' — nomenclature discrepancy, stored per the SKU/datasheet as type-4.

licenses.notes: Network stack perpetual license, embedded with hardware, included at no separate selection. Tier locked to the switch's -E suffix. Pairs with the mandatory-attach DNA term subscription (OG License matrix, Table 10 datasheet).

Network stack perpetual license, embedded with hardware, included at no separate selection. Tier locked to the switch's -A suffix. Pairs with the mandatory-attach DNA term subscription (OG License matrix, Table 10 datasheet).

groups._comment: Named shared compatibility SETS. A switch references a group by id and inherits the whole set.

network_module_groups.notes: Non-PXG modular C9200 models (24T, 24P, 24PB, 48T, 48P, 48PL, 48PB). C9200-NM-2Y/2Q are PXG-only and excluded; C9200-NM-4G is not on the PXG models (OG Table 3 footnotes).
C9200-24PXG / C9200-48PXG only. Adds the PXG-only 25G (NM-2Y) and 40G (NM-2Q) modules; C9200-NM-4G is not supported on PXG models (OG Table 3 footnotes).

power_supply_groups.notes: Cisco Catalyst 9200 (modular uplink) Config-6 AC PSU family (OG Table 5). AC only (DC PWR-C6-715WDC intentionally omitted). Which member is a valid PRIMARY, the default shipped PSU, and the PoE budget per (primary,secondary) pair are switch-specific and live on the model entry (Pass 2).

Cisco Catalyst 9200L (fixed uplink) Config-5 AC PSU family (OG Table 5). AC only (DC PWR-C5-715WDC intentionally omitted). Distinct SKUs from the C9200 Config-6 family (shallower chassis); kept as its own named group. Per-model valid_primary/default/PoE budget live on the model entry (Pass 2).

stack_cable_groups.notes: StackWise-160 data stacking for C9200 modular models via C9200-STACK-KIT (two data stack adapters + one cable, 50 cm default). Type-4 data stack cables (datasheet Tables 5-6).

StackWise-80 data stacking for C9200L fixed-uplink models via C9200L-STACK-KIT. Same Type-4 STACK-T4 cables as the C9200 group but a distinct kit; kept as its own named group per project convention (datasheet Tables 5-6).

term_variable.notes: Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE; the pick resolves the concrete -<n>Y subscription SKU. Subscription is mandatory; tier locked to -E.
Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE; the pick resolves the concrete -<n>Y subscription SKU. Subscription is mandatory; tier locked to -A.
Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE; the pick resolves the concrete -<n>Y subscription SKU. Subscription is mandatory; tier locked to -E.
Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE; the pick resolves the concrete -<n>Y subscription SKU. Subscription is mandatory; tier locked to -A.

license_groups.applies_to: 48-port C9200L (fixed uplink) models, Essentials tier (-E suffix).

term_variable.notes: Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE; the pick resolves the concrete -<n>Y subscription SKU. Subscription is mandatory; tier locked to -E.

license_groups.applies_to: 48-port C9200L (fixed uplink) models, Advantage tier (-A suffix).

term_variable.notes: Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE; the pick resolves the concrete -<n>Y subscription SKU. Subscription is mandatory; tier locked to -A.

license_groups.applies_to: 24-port C9200L (fixed uplink) models, Essentials tier (-E suffix).

term_variable.notes: Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE; the pick resolves the concrete -<n>Y subscription SKU. Subscription is mandatory; tier locked to -E.

license_groups.applies_to: 24-port C9200L (fixed uplink) models, Advantage tier (-A suffix).

term_variable.notes: Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE; the pick resolves the concrete -<n>Y subscription SKU. Subscription is mandatory; tier locked to -A.
